S0001.title: Arrow, K. J. (1951). Social Choice and Individual Values
S0001.discovery_method: Academic search - referenced in political theory courses

S0002.title: Black, D. (1958). The Theory of Committees and Elections
S0002.discovery_method: Google Scholar search - related to Condorcet

C0001.initial_statement: Approval voting prevents minority tyranny because it allows expressing support for multiple candidates

updates.update: Approval voting may not fully prevent gerrymandering in multi-winner elections
updates.reasoning: Approval voting solves some issues but not all; ranked choice may be better for some scenarios
updates.inspiration: Review of Lijphart's democratic peace research

evidence_supporting: Approval voting allows voters to support multiple candidates, preventing vote-splitting
Approval voting has been used successfully in professional societies and organizations
C0001.reasoning: Based on Arrow's theorem showing all systems have trade-offs, approval voting allows expressing full preferences

P0001.prompt: Analyze voting systems for minority protection
P0001.context: Based on Arrow and Lijphart research

P0002.prompt: Evaluate approval voting vs ranked choice
